This biographical article relating to Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Saima_Manzoor&action=edit).

(born 24 March 1981), also known as **Saima Waqas**, is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1) She was the bronze medalists at the [2006 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2006_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2006 South Asian Games") in the mixed doubles and team events. Manzoor won the 2016 [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament in the women's doubles event.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["Players: Saima Manzoor"](http://bwfbadminton.com/player/53295/saima-manzoor). *bwfbadminton.com*. [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation"). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20181225181559/https://bwfbadminton.com/player/53295/saima-waqas) from the original on 25 December 2018. Retrieved 12 November 2016.

["Rizwan, Palwasha win badminton titles"](https://www.thenews.com.pk/print/159018-Rizwan-Palwasha-win-badminton-titles). *www.thenews.com.pk*. [The News International](/wiki/The_News_International "The News International"). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20181225181555/https://www.thenews.com.pk/print/159018-Rizwan-Palwasha-win-badminton-titles%20) from the original on 25 December 2018. Retrieved 12 November 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani badminton player (born 1981)

Saima Manzoor

| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") | | | | Representing  [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") | | | | [South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the South Asian Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2004 Islamabad](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2004_South_Asian_Federation_Games "Badminton at the 2004 South Asian Federation Games") | Mixed doubles | | Bronze medal – third place | 2004 Islamabad | Women's team | | Bronze medal – third place | [2006 Colombo](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2006_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2006 South Asian Games") | Mixed doubles | | Bronze medal – third place | 2006 Colombo | Women's team | | |

**Saima Manzoor** (born 24 March 1981), also known as **Saima Waqas**, is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1) She was the bronze medalists at the [2006 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2006_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2006 South Asian Games") in the mixed doubles and team events. Manzoor won the 2016 [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament in the women's doubles event.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
